commit.message:
keep media-bar visible upon bulk processing completion

Users need static visual confirmation when a bulk processing run finishes.
Leaving the primary progress bar on the screen clarifies that the entire
operation has concluded, without the clutter of secondary and transient tracking
bars lingering alongside it. Strict event ordering is needed during finalization
The frontend must receive the absolute final progress updates before the cleanup
signal arrives, avoiding out-of-order execution artifacts on the UI.

∙ Emit a completion lifecycle event to selectively prune frontend progress bars
∙ Synchronize progress batch flush emission to guarantee logical arrival order

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-Authored-By: Gemini <noreply@deepmind.google>

commit.message:
docs(agents): 补充默认简洁回答规则 (#27)

## Why
- 近期主力模型更容易输出过长回答，需要在全局约束中补充默认回答风格。
- 将“优先回答问题本身、避免无请求展开”写入规范，降低日常对话的冗长程度。

## What
- 在 `AGENTS.md` 的“沟通与称呼”章节新增默认回答风格约束。
- 明确要求默认简洁回答，并在未被明确要求时不展开背景、替代方案或额外建议。

## Testing
- 未运行自动化测试；本次仅修改文档规则。

commit.message:
refactor(runtime): split PREOP configuration from activation

Summary:
- add explicit PREOP session lifecycle with `phase/0`,
  `configure_slave/2`, `activate/0`, and `await_operational/1`
- generalize `EtherCAT.Slave.Driver` around logical signals,
  PREOP mailbox steps, and distributed clocks
- refactor slave process-data planning to map signals onto PDO/SM
  layout with explicit validation for unsupported cross-domain SM use
- update examples, docs, and tests to the new public and driver APIs

Rationale:
- align the runtime with the EtherCAT startup sequence where
  mailbox configuration and PDO/FMMU setup happen in PREOP before OP
- make the driver abstraction less Beckhoff-terminal-specific and more
  honest about the protocol layers the master actually configures
- support both static startup and dynamic discovery/configuration
  without keeping misleading compatibility surfaces

Validation:
- mix compile
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Animate the prince with sprites

Co-authored-by: GPT-5.3-Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
align Anki return shortcut with native D shortcut + free ESC for UI modal escape

Escape was overloaded: it forced an Anki return even when users expected it to
dismiss in-app overlays. Langkit already has a safe integration point for
Anki's native deck navigation, so returning should follow that path instead.

∙ remove the WebView-level Escape shortcut that bypassed in-app modal behavior
∙ rely on the existing moveToState deck wrapper so Anki's D cleanly hides
  Langkit before deck navigation

follow up of 4167616

commit.message:
refactor(domain): separate layout planning from runtime

Summary:
- move Domain.Config into its own file and add a pure Domain.Layout
  module for registration and cycle-plan preparation
- rename the domain period API to period_ms and align examples,
  docs, and master normalization with the clearer option name
- simplify Domain runtime state and make stop_cycling/1
  idempotent and consistently named with start_cycling/1

Rationale:
- the previous Domain module mixed config schema, layout planning,
  and cyclic runtime logic in one file and used less precise naming
- separating config and layout makes the LRW image rules easier to
  verify while keeping the runtime state machine smaller and clearer

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/domain/layout_test.exs test/ethercat/domain_test.exs
- mix test

commit.message:
refactor(dc): separate init planning from bus io

Summary:
- split DC initialization into Snapshot and InitPlan helpers with
  pure chain-planning logic
- simplify DC bus I/O to one snapshot read transaction per slave and
  explicit checked writes for offsets, delays, and filter reset
- update master and architecture docs and add planner unit tests

Rationale:
- the old DC path mixed protocol math, topology assumptions, and bus
  I/O in one module and silently ignored init write failures
- a pure planning layer keeps the spec-facing logic inspectable and
  testable without reintroducing module injection

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/dc/init_plan_test.exs
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Critical fixes for employee workflow:

✅ Fixed call logging - Now saves to Supabase (was using non-existent logCall)
✅ Added workStage tracking (To Do / Doing / Done) synced to cloud
✅ Call Tomorrow quick action (sets follow-up date, marks not answered)
✅ Fixed Tailwind dynamic classes for production builds
✅ Enhanced call outcome buttons with explicit styling
✅ Added Work Progress tracker visible to managers

Fixes:
- Call logs were not being saved (logCall → addCallLog)
- Dynamic Tailwind classes missing in production
- Missing employee work stage tracking
- No quick action for common "call tomorrow" workflow

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: fanbe-hostinger
repository.description: FanBe Group - Production Website (Hostinger Deployment)

commit.message:
refactor(master): simplify spec-aligned startup

Summary:
- remove module injection and make Master call Bus, DC, Domain,
  and Slave directly
- restructure startup into explicit EtherCAT phases for discovery,
  station assignment, init reset, DC init, domain start, and
  activation
- add a hardware Livebook for EL1809/EL2809 loopback latency,
  throughput, and scheduler-priority benchmarks

Rationale:
- the injected module layer added indirection without any real
  runtime value and made the startup flow harder to verify against
  the EtherCAT spec
- explicit phase functions make the configuration sequence easier
  to read, reason about, and compare with the reference material

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix: Hostinger deployment cache busting with unique build IDs & version endpoint

Implements comprehensive deployment fix:

✅ Unique build IDs per deployment (timestamp + random)
✅ Clean rebuild script (npm run build:clean)
✅ Version endpoint at /api/version
✅ Strict cache control for index.html and version.json
✅ Immutable caching for hashed assets

Fixes production serving stale JS bundles issue.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: fanbe-hostinger

commit.message:
stabilize preflight status under fast cached integrity checks

Decode caching made some preflight runs complete so quickly that check state
flipped to idle before throttled progress events finished draining.
The UI could then show "Processing Status" while check bars were still visible,
which looked broken and obscured real check activity.

∙ derive a visual check state from run state OR active check-* progress bars
∙ scope this logic to check-probe/check-decode so processing bars stay isolated
∙ keep processing state authoritative over check state in status transitions
∙ only return to idle text after visual check state is fully cleared

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
refactor(bus): centralize scheduling and link redundancy

Summary:
- move scheduling, queueing, deadline handling, and reply routing
  into EtherCAT.Bus
- add Submission/InFlight lifecycle records and Bus.Link single-port
  and redundant adapters
- remove Command and transaction_queue/2, and update telemetry,
  tests, and docs to the current API

Rationale:
- keep bus policy in one gen_statem so single-port and redundant
  paths share the same behavior
- give realtime traffic strict priority without mixing it with
  reliable frames
- align reference docs and execution plans with the implemented
  architecture to reduce drift

Validation:
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
chore(github-pr-issue): 精简 skill 提示并移除 read_pr 封装 (#26)

## Why
- 随着 LLM 模型能力提升，模型已经能较好理解并使用 `gh` 命令行工具，无需在 skill 提示词中保留大量细碎的命令用法说明。
- 继续维护额外的 Python 封装和命令级使用细节，会增加提示与实现复杂度，但收益已经很有限。

## What
- 删除 `read_pr.py` 相关说明与脚本实现。
- 删除 `--body-file` 等过细的 `gh` CLI 用法示例，保留更高层的操作约定。
- 将 `github-pr-issue` skill 收敛为更轻量的规范，降低提示词长度与维护成本。

## Testing
- 未运行自动化测试；本次变更仅涉及文档调整与脚本删除。
- 已使用 `git diff origin/master...HEAD` 复核改动范围。

## Notes
- 这次调整的核心判断是：优先信任当前 LLM 对 `gh` CLI 的通用操作能力，而不是继续在提示词中堆积命令细节或维护额外封装层。

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

introduce bridge-session seam for Homey runtime

Add a typed bridge-session module and route app lifecycle/client ownership through the default session (main), then update bridge/node drivers and devices to resolve runtime client access via getBridgeSession first with legacy fallbacks retained. Includes harness/runtime tests that exercise the session path and docs/plan sync for Phase 5 status.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): align Linux packaging with supported targets

Summary:
- replace the broken ARM64 electron-builder deb path with a native dpkg-deb packager
- stage the extracted desktop shell, launcher shim, and ripgrep consistently for local, Docker, and CI builds
- replace the old multi-distro CI matrix with x86_64 and arm64 package builds plus optional smoke tests
- update build docs and ignore generated package outputs and local scratch trees

Rationale:
- keep the repository aligned with the real extracted Codex runtime contract instead of the earlier aspirational matrix
- make CI build only the package targets that the repo can actually produce and validate today
- avoid committing generated binaries while keeping the Linux packaging flow reproducible

Tests:
- ruby -e 'require yaml; YAML.load_file(.github/workflows/ci.yml)'
- bash -n scripts/build-linux-deb.sh docker/build.sh scripts/ci-build.sh codex-linux-fork/build.sh
- orbctl Ubuntu ARM64 VM launch validation for the installed .deb and AppImage until app-server handshake reached connected
- docker build --platform linux/amd64 -f docker/Dockerfile -t codex-build:amd64-ci .
- docker run --rm --platform linux/amd64 codex-build:amd64-ci ... TARGET_ARCH=amd64 bash /build/scripts/build-linux-deb.sh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(macos): preserve launchd PATH for docker CLI

Summary:
- augment the macOS daemon PATH with /opt/homebrew/bin and
  /usr/local/bin before host-side docker commands run
- write the augmented PATH into generated launchd plists so login
  daemons inherit standard user-installed binary directories
- add regression tests for PATH extension and launchd plist output

Rationale:
- launchd starts coastd with a stripped PATH, which breaks docker
  subprocesses when the CLI is installed outside the system paths
- standard user-installed binary directories cover Homebrew and
  symlinked OrbStack/docker installs without hardcoding app internals

Tests:
- cargo fmt --all
- COAST_SKIP_UI_BUILD=1 cargo test -p coast-daemon
  test_extend_path_entries_appends_only_missing_candidates --lib
- COAST_SKIP_UI_BUILD=1 cargo test -p coast-cli
  test_generate_launchd_plist_content
- COAST_SKIP_UI_BUILD=1 cargo test -p coast-cli
  test_extend_path_entries_appends_only_missing_candidates
- COAST_SKIP_UI_BUILD=1 cargo build -p coast-daemon --bin coastd-dev
  -p coast-cli --bin coast-dev
- PATH="/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin"
  /Users/af/symphony/coasts/target/debug/coast-dev build
  (from /Users/af/symphony)
- PATH="/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin"
  /Users/af/symphony/coasts/target/debug/coast-dev run pr-check-1
  (from /Users/af/symphony)
- COAST_SKIP_UI_BUILD=1 cargo clippy -p coast-daemon -p coast-cli
  --all-targets -- -D warnings
  (fails due pre-existing warnings in untouched files)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
